title: Is the quality of our coastal waters good enough for swimming?
subtitle: In 2025, 87.4% of bathing waters in the EU were classified as ‘excellent’, a figure that has remained between 81% and 89% since 2010. You’ll enjoy the cleanest swim in the coastal bathing waters of Cyprus, Lithuania and Slovenia, but Poland, Belgium, Finland and Estonia still have some way to go.
textNote: Some countries may have lower scores for ‘excellent’ quality, whilst still having ‘good’ quality waters.
